P101.itemDescription: specialization of a person or organization; see P106 for the occupation

P452.itemDescription: specific industry of company or organization

P1416.itemDescription: organization that a person or organization is affiliated with (not necessarily member of or employed by)

P400.itemDescription: platform for which a work was developed or released, or the specific platform version of a software product

P361.itemDescription: object of which the subject is a part (if this subject is already part of object A which is a part of object B, then please only make the subject part of object A), inverse property of "has part" (P527, see also "has parts of the class" (P2670))

P276.itemDescription: location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to indicate the containing administrative entity, P8138 for statistical entities, or P706 for geographic entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object

P366.itemDescription: main use of the subject (includes current and former usage)

P1414.itemDescription: framework or toolkit a program uses to display the graphical user interface

P6001.itemDescription: qualifier to a specific group of people, members of a group or organization

P156.itemDescription: immediately following item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has been replaced, e.g. political offices, use "replaced by" (P1366)]

P4428.itemDescription: implementation of a standard, program, specification or programming language

P3985.itemDescription: programming language which is supported by this programming tool

P559.itemDescription: the feature (intersecting road, train station, etc.) at the end of a linear feature

P2702.itemDescription: particular manner of distribution of a data set (database or file) that is publicly available

P749.itemDescription: parent organization or unit of an organization or unit, opposite of child organization or unit (P355); use instance of (P31) to distinguish organization (Q43229) and organization unit (Q10387680)

P4878.itemDescription: symbolism of an artistic element; can also be used as a qualifier for "depicts" (P180) statements

P7162.itemDescription: what other cases this case consolidated

P3931.itemDescription: person or organisation who holds the copyright of a work according to the Berne Convention

P4934.itemDescription: value of the subject item cannot be measured directly or can be calculated from the following measurands or attributes

P50.itemDescription: main creator(s) of a written work (use on works, not humans); use P2093 (author name string) when Wikidata item is unknown or does not exist

P1889.itemDescription: item that is different from another item, with which it may be confused

P747.itemDescription: item that is an edition or translation of this item

P131.itemLabel: located in the administrative territorial entity
P131.itemDescription: the item is located on the territory of the following administrative entity. Use P276 for specifying locations that are non-administrative places and for items about events. Use P1382 if the item falls only partially into the administrative entity.

P751.itemDescription: feature introduced by this version of a product item

P31.itemDescription: type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different from P279 (subclass of); for example: K2 is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain

P737.itemDescription: the subject (person, idea, etc.) was influenced or inspired by this object entity, e.g. "Heidegger was influenced by Aristotle"

P2341.itemDescription: place or ethnic group where a language, art genre, cultural tradition or expression, cooking style or food, or biological species or variety is found (or was originally found)

P1557.itemDescription: inherent and characteristic embodiment of a given concept

P98.itemDescription: person who checks and corrects a work (such as a book, newspaper, academic journal, etc.) to comply with a rules of certain genre. Also applies to person who establishes the text of an ancient written work or manuscript.

P2701.itemDescription: file format, compression type, or ontology used in a file

P12328.itemDescription: item simulated, imitated, or made to appear real by this item

P126.itemDescription: person or organization in charge of keeping the subject (for instance an infrastructure) in functioning order

P7228.itemDescription: status of any access restrictions on the object, collection, or materials. Indicates whether the material is available to the general public or not

P155.itemDescription: immediately prior item in a series of which the subject is a part, preferably use as qualifier of P179 [if the subject has replaced the preceding item, e.g. political offices, use "replaces" (P1365)]

P275.itemDescription: license under which this copyrighted work is released

P4586.itemDescription: company releasing or distributing a font or typeface

P4290.itemDescription: official app for this organization or other entity (a downloadable application for Android, iOS, etc.)

P629.itemDescription: is an edition or translation of this entity

P748.itemDescription: who appointed the person to the position, can be used as a qualifier

P279.itemDescription: this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances of this item are instances of that item; different from P31 (instance of), e.g.: volcano is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain

P9419.itemDescription: institution holding the subject’s personal library

P4967.itemDescription: prior event or achievement that a person or team needs to complete before joining or obtaining the item topic, e.g. a qualifying round of a sports event, a lower-level course in an educational program, or a lower level of a professional qualification

P6977.itemDescription: this property connects a review to the work it reviews

P463.itemDescription: organization, club or musical group to which the subject belongs. Do not use for membership in ethnic or social groups, nor for holding a political position, such as a member of parliament (use P39 for that)

P1365.itemDescription: person, state or item replaced. Use "structure replaces" (P1398) for structures. Use "follows" (P155) if the previous item was not replaced or predecessor and successor are identical

P10645.itemDescription: the subject position reports to this position

P1389.itemDescription: certification for a product, qualify with P1001 (“applies to jurisdiction”) if needed

P138.itemDescription: entity or event that inspired the subject's name, or namesake (in at least one language). Qualifier "applies to name" (P5168) can be used to indicate which one

P437.itemDescription: method (or type) of distribution for the subject

P13308.itemDescription: software that was used to create this media or work

P1547.itemDescription: subject software depends on object software

P8131.itemDescription: computer language of which an item with this property is a dialect (a relatively small variation)

P2675.itemDescription: the intellectual work to which the subsequent work is a direct reply

P178.itemDescription: organization or person that developed the item

P1552.itemDescription: inherent or distinguishing quality or feature of the entity. Use a more specific property when possible

P2283.itemDescription: item or concept used by the subject or in the operation (see also instrument [P1303] and armament [P520])

P5460.itemDescription: confers degree, honor, award, prize, title, certificate or medal denoting achievement to a person or organization

P1534.itemDescription: qualifier used together with the end date qualifier (P582) to specify the reason for the end

P527.itemDescription: part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).

P8111.itemDescription: unit in which a quantity is measured as recommended by a standard (SI, ISO, IEC, etc.)

P111.itemDescription: value of a physical property expressed as number multiplied by a unit

P767.itemLabel: contributor to the creative work or subject
P767.itemDescription: person or organization that contributed to a subject: co-creator of a creative work or subject

P144.itemDescription: the work(s) or inputs used as the basis for subject item; for fictional analog use P1074

P5402.itemDescription: lexeme in the same language with the same spelling as this one

P805.itemDescription: (qualifying) item that describes the relation identified in this statement

P4330.itemDescription: contents, item or substance located within this wrapping item, but not part of this receptacle or container; does not describe the ingredients or components of this container

P12913.itemDescription: class that includes the object(s) to which this occurrence (or class of occurrence) occurs or occurred

P793.itemDescription: significant or notable events associated with the subject

P1072.itemDescription: file format a program can open and read

P5135.itemDescription: instances of the item have a greater value than corresponding instances of the object, for the given measure

P1382.itemDescription: object that partially overlaps with the subject in its instances, parts, or members

P1366.itemDescription: other person or item which continues the item by replacing it in its role. Use P156 ("followed by") if the item is not replaced nor identical, but adds to the series (e.g. books in a series).

P1073.itemDescription: file format a program can create and/or write to

P2541.itemDescription: geographic area or jurisdiction an organisation or industry operates in, serves, or has responsibility for

P355.itemDescription: child organization/unit of a organization/unit; for companies, generally a fully owned separate corp., see business division (P199); opposite of parent org./unit (P749); use instance of (P31) to distinguish org. (Q43229) and org. unit (Q10387680)

This includes qualifications that one needs to obtain in order to join the industry or qualifications that one obtains after a certain level of experience in the industry in order to progress further in their career.

P3301.itemDescription: channel, network, website or service that broadcast this item over radio, television or the Internet

P6531.itemDescription: to be used as reference to location, condition, variant where upon the subject property object statement is based

P5008.itemLabel: on focus list of Wikimedia project
P5008.itemDescription: property to indicate that an item is of particular interest for a Wikimedia project. This property does not add notability. Items should not be created with this property if they are not notable for Wikidata. See also P6104, P972, P2354.

P790.itemDescription: item is approved by other item(s) [qualifier: statement is approved by other item(s)]

P460.itemDescription: this item is said to be the same as that item, though this may be uncertain or disputed

P4543.itemDescription: substance that's listed as ingredient on the packaging of the product; use P1545 to qualify the order of the listed ingredients

P408.itemDescription: software engine employed by the subject item

P3729.itemDescription: lower rank or level in a ranked hierarchy like sport league, military ranks. If there are several possible, list each one and qualify with "criterion used" (P1013), avoid using ranks and date qualifiers. For sports leagues/taxa, use specific properties instead.

P2361.itemDescription: online service or online service provider tied to an item

P1537.itemDescription: thing that is significantly influenced by this cause, but does not directly result from it. See 'Help:Modeling causes' for examples and discussion.

P2567.itemDescription: document is amended by specified other document

P750.itemDescription: distributor of a creative work; distributor for a record label; news agency; film distributor

P9073.itemDescription: value suitable for "stated in" (P248) when property is used in references to refer to the primary publication/website/database associated with the property

P180.itemDescription: entity visually depicted in an image, literarily described in a work, or otherwise incorporated into an audiovisual or other medium; see also P921, 'main subject'

P2061.itemDescription: image width to height ratio of flags, films, photographs, and other images

P166.itemDescription: award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work

P8693.itemDescription: fan group of a celebrity, musical group, artist, work, etc.

P291.itemDescription: geographical place of publication of the edition (use 1st edition when referring to works)

P6195.itemDescription: is supported under the funding scheme

P4185.itemDescription: identifying element typically depicted as accompanying or worn by this religious figure, hero, fictional or historical character

P162.itemDescription: person(s) who produced the film, musical work, theatrical production, etc. (for film, this does not include executive producers, associate producers, etc.) [for production company, use P272, video games - use P178]

P11558.itemDescription: what the subject is the digital equivalent of

P88.itemDescription: person or organization that commissioned this work

P3113.itemDescription: expected part that the item does not have (for qualities, use P6477)

P10476.itemDescription: this item uniquely and purposefully distinguishes instances of this class from each other

P4788.itemDescription: data storage device supported by a machine (e.g. camera or mobile phone)

P2846.itemDescription: describes disabled accessibility of location or event
